description_long: This is a wooden fence section composed of three vertical posts connected by two horizontal rails, forming a right-angled corner. The rails and posts are made of natural wood, with the rails slightly curved at one end, giving the fence a rustic and handcrafted appearance. The wood grain is visible, and the color is a warm, medium brown, typical of untreated or lightly finished timber. The fence lacks any decorative elements or post caps, emphasizing a simple, utilitarian design. The structure is open, with significant gaps between the rails and posts, making it suitable for marking boundaries rather than providing security or privacy. This type of fence is commonly found in parks, gardens, or along pathways in urban and suburban environments.
description: A simple, rustic wooden fence section with three posts and two horizontal rails, forming a right-angled corner. The wood is medium brown with visible grain and no decorative features.
description_view_0: The fence is seen from an angle showing both the long and short sections, highlighting the right-angled corner and the curved ends of the rails.
description_view_1: A side view emphasizing the short section of the fence and the curvature of the rails at the end.
description_view_2: A straight-on view of the longer section, showing the alignment of the posts and rails with the corner visible on the left.
description_view_3: A top-down view that clearly displays the L-shaped layout and the spacing between the rails and posts.
attribute_top_design_pattern: flat with slight curvature at ends
